translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now these are the names of the sons of Israel, who came into Egypt (every man and his household came with Jacob):

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the souls who came out of Jacob’s body were seventy souls, and Joseph was in Egypt already.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joseph died, as did all his brothers, and all that generation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel were fruitful, and increased abundantly, and multiplied, and grew exceedingly mighty; and the land was filled with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who didn’t know Joseph.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to his people, “Behold,  the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Come, let us deal wisely with them, lest they multiply, and it happen that when any war breaks out, they also join themselves to our enemies, and fight against us, and escape out of the land.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to afflict them with their burdens. They built storage cities for Pharaoh: Pithom and Raamses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and the more they spread out. They were grieved because of the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Egyptians ruthlessly made the children of Israel serve,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they made their lives bitter with hard service, in mortar and in brick, and in all kinds of service in the field, all their service, in which they ruthlessly made them serve.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Egypt spoke to the Hebrew midwives, of whom the name of the one was Shiphrah, and the name of the other Puah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he said, “When you perform the duty of a midwife to the Hebrew women, and see them on the birth stool; if it is a son, then you shall kill him; but if it is a daughter, then she shall live.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the midwives feared God,  and didn’t do what the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the baby boys alive.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Egypt called for the midwives, and said to them, “Why have you done this thing, and have saved the boys alive?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The midwives said to Pharaoh, “Because the Hebrew women aren’t like the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous, and give birth before the midwife comes to them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God dealt well with the midwives, and the people multiplied, and grew very mighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because the midwives feared God, he gave them families.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh commanded all his people, saying, “You shall cast every son who is born into the river, and every daughter you shall save alive.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man of the house of Levi went and took a daughter of Levi as his wife.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The woman conceived, and bore a son. When she saw that he was a fine child, she hid him three months.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she could no longer hide him, she took a papyrus basket for him, and coated it with tar and with pitch. She put the child in it, and laid it in the reeds by the river’s bank.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His sister stood far off, to see what would be done to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh’s daughter came down to bathe at the river. Her maidens walked along by the riverside. She saw the basket among the reeds, and sent her servant to get it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She opened it, and saw the child, and behold, the baby cried. She had compassion on him, and said, “This is one of the Hebrews’ children.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, “Should I go and call a nurse for you from the Hebrew women, that she may nurse the child for you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Go.” The maiden went and called the child’s mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child away, and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages.” The woman took the child, and nursed it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The child grew, and she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. She named him Moses,  and said, “Because I drew him out of the water.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In those days, when Moses had grown up, he went out to his brothers, and looked at their burdens. He saw an Egyptian striking a Hebrew, one of his brothers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He looked this way and that way, and when he saw that there was no one, he killed the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went out the second day, and behold, two men of the Hebrews were fighting with each other. He said to him who did the wrong, “Why do you strike your fellow?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you plan to kill me, as you killed the Egyptian?” Moses was afraid, and said, “Surely this thing is known.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when Pharaoh heard this thing, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and lived in the land of Midian, and he sat down by a well.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters. They came and drew water, and filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The shepherds came and drove them away; but Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came to Reuel, their father, he said, “How is it that you have returned so early today?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said, “An Egyptian delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds, and moreover he drew water for us, and watered the flock.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to his daughters, “Where is he? Why is it that you have left the man? Call him, that he may eat bread.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses was content to dwell with the man. He gave Moses Zipporah, his daughter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She bore a son, and he named him Gershom,  for he said, “I have lived as a foreigner in a foreign land.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the course of those many days, the king of Egypt died, and the children of Israel sighed because of the bondage, and they cried, and their cry came up to God because of the bondage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God saw the children of Israel, and God was concerned about them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Moses was keeping the flock of Jethro, his father-in-law, the priest of Midian, and he led the flock to the back of the wilderness, and came to God’s mountain, to Horeb.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s  angel appeared to him in a flame of fire out of the middle of a bush. He looked, and behold, the bush burned with fire, and the bush was not consumed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “I will turn aside now, and see this great sight, why the bush is not burned.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Yahweh saw that he turned aside to see, God called to him out of the middle of the bush, and said, “Moses! Moses!” He said, “Here I am.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Don’t come close. Take your sandals off of your feet, for the place you are standing on is holy ground.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover he said, “I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.” Moses hid his face; for he was afraid to look at God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said, “I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters, for I know their sorrows.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land to a good and large land, to a land flowing with milk and honey; to the place of the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Amorite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now, behold, the cry of the children of Israel has come to me. Moreover I have seen the oppression with which the Egyptians oppress them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Come now therefore, and I will send you to Pharaoh, that you may bring my people, the children of Israel, out of Egypt.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to God, “Who am I, that I should go to Pharaoh, and that I should bring the children of Israel out of Egypt?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Certainly I will be with you. This will be the token to you, that I have sent you: when you have brought the people out of Egypt, you shall serve God on this mountain.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to God, “Behold, when I come to the children of Israel, and tell them, ‘The God of your fathers has sent me to you;’ and they ask me, ‘What is his name?’ What should I tell them?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God said to Moses, “I AM WHO I AM,” and he said, “You shall tell the children of Israel this: ‘I AM has sent me to you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God said moreover to Moses, “You shall tell the children of Israel this, ‘Yahweh, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has sent me to you.’ This is my name forever, and this is my memorial to all generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go, and gather the elders of Israel together, and tell them, ‘Yahweh, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, has appeared to me, saying, “I have surely visited you, and seen that which is done to you in Egypt;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I have said, I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt to the land of the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Amorite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, to a land flowing with milk and honey.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will listen to your voice, and you shall come, you and the elders of Israel, to the king of Egypt, and you shall tell him, ‘Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us. Now please let us go three days’ journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to Yahweh, our God.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I know that the king of Egypt won’t give you permission to go, no, not by a mighty hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will reach out my hand and strike Egypt with all my wonders which I will do among them, and after that he will let you go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will give this people favor in the sight of the Egyptians, and it will happen that when you go, you shall not go empty-handed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But every woman shall ask of her neighbor, and of her who visits her house, jewels of silver, jewels of gold, and clothing; and you shall put them on your sons, and on your daughters. You shall plunder the Egyptians.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses answered, “But, behold, they will not believe me, nor listen to my voice; for they will say, ‘Yahweh has not appeared to you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to him, “What is that in your hand?” He said, “A rod.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Throw it on the ground.” He threw it on the ground, and it became a snake; and Moses ran away from it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand, and take it by the tail.” He stretched out his hand, and took hold of it, and it became a rod in his hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “That they may believe that Yahweh, the God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has appeared to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said furthermore to him, “Now put your hand inside your cloak.” He put his hand inside his cloak, and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous, as white as snow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Put your hand inside your cloak again.” He put his hand inside his cloak again, and when he took it out of his cloak, behold, it had turned again as his other flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “It will happen, if they will not believe you or listen to the voice of the first sign, that they will believe the voice of the latter sign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It will happen, if they will not believe even these two signs or listen to your voice, that you shall take of the water of the river, and pour it on the dry land. The water which you take out of the river will become blood on the dry land.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Yahweh, “O Lord,  I am not eloquent, neither before now, nor since you have spoken to your servant; for I am slow of speech, and of a slow tongue.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to him, “Who made man’s mouth? Or who makes one mute, or deaf, or seeing, or blind? Isn’t it I, Yahweh?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore go, and I will be with your mouth, and teach you what you shall speak.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Oh, Lord, please send someone else.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s anger burned against Moses, and he said, “What about Aaron, your brother, the Levite? I know that he can speak well. Also, behold, he comes out to meet you. When he sees you, he will be glad in his heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall speak to him, and put the words in his mouth. I will be with your mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what you shall do.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will be your spokesman to the people; and it will happen, that he will be to you a mouth, and you will be to him as God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take this rod in your hand, with which you shall do the signs.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses went and returned to Jethro his father-in-law, and said to him, “Please let me go and return to my brothers who are in Egypt, and see whether they are still alive.” Jethro said to Moses, “Go in peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses in Midian, “Go, return into Egypt; for all the men who sought your life are dead.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses took his wife and his sons, and set them on a donkey, and he returned to the land of Egypt. Moses took God’s rod in his hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “When you go back into Egypt, see that you do before Pharaoh all the wonders which I have put in your hand, but I will harden his heart and he will not let the people go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall tell Pharaoh, ‘Yahweh says, Israel is my son, my firstborn,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I have said to you, “Let my son go, that he may serve me”; and you have refused to let him go. Behold, I will kill your son, your firstborn.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the way at a lodging place, Yahweh met Moses and wanted to kill him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Zipporah took a flint, and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it at his feet; and she said, “Surely you are a bridegroom of blood to me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So he let him alone. Then she said, “You are a bridegroom of blood,” because of the circumcision.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Aaron, “Go into the wilderness to meet Moses.” He went, and met him on God’s mountain, and kissed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses told Aaron all Yahweh’s words with which he had sent him, and all the signs with which he had instructed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron spoke all the words which Yahweh had spoken to Moses, and did the signs in the sight of the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people believed, and when they heard that Yahweh had visited the children of Israel, and that he had seen their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshiped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Afterward Moses and Aaron came, and said to Pharaoh, “This is what Yahweh, the God of Israel, says, ‘Let my people go, that they may hold a feast to me in the wilderness.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh said, “Who is Yahweh, that I should listen to his voice to let Israel go? I don’t know Yahweh, and moreover I will not let Israel go.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said, “The God of the Hebrews has met with us. Please let us go three days’ journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to Yahweh, our God, lest he fall on us with pestilence, or with the sword.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Egypt said to them, “Why do you, Moses and Aaron, take the people from their work? Get back to your burdens!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh said, “Behold, the people of the land are now many, and you make them rest from their burdens.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The same day Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people, and their officers, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall no longer give the people straw to make brick, as before. Let them go and gather straw for themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The number of the bricks, which they made before, you require from them. You shall not diminish anything of it, for they are idle; therefore they cry, saying, ‘Let us go and sacrifice to our God.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let heavier work be laid on the men, that they may labor therein; and don’t let them pay any attention to lying words.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The taskmasters of the people went out, and their officers, and they spoke to the people, saying, “This is what Pharaoh says: ‘I will not give you straw.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go yourselves, get straw where you can find it, for nothing of your work shall be diminished.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the people were scattered abroad throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The taskmasters were urgent saying, “Fulfill your work quota daily, as when there was straw!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The officers of the children of Israel, whom Pharaoh’s taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, and demanded, “Why haven’t you fulfilled your quota both yesterday and today, in making brick as before?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the officers of the children of Israel came and cried to Pharaoh, saying, “Why do you deal this way with your servants?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No straw is given to your servants, and they tell us, ‘Make brick!’ and behold, your servants are beaten; but the fault is in your own people.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said, “You are idle! You are idle! Therefore you say, ‘Let us go and sacrifice to Yahweh.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go therefore now, and work, for no straw shall be given to you, yet you shall deliver the same number of bricks!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The officers of the children of Israel saw that they were in trouble, when it was said, “You shall not diminish anything from your daily quota of bricks!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They met Moses and Aaron, who stood in the way, as they came out from Pharaoh:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they said to them, “May Yahweh look at you, and judge, because you have made us a stench to be abhorred in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his servants, to put a sword in their hand to kill us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses returned to Yahweh, and said, “Lord, why have you brought trouble on this people? Why is it that you have sent me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For since I came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has brought trouble on this people; and you have not rescued your people at all.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh, for by a strong hand he shall let them go, and by a strong hand he shall drive them out of his land.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God spoke to Moses, and said to him, “I am Yahweh;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, as God Almighty; but by my name Yahweh I was not known to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their travels, in which they lived as aliens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover I have heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians keep in bondage, and I have remembered my covenant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore tell the children of Israel, ‘I am Yahweh, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will rid you out of their bondage, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm, and with great judgments:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will take you to me for a people, and I will be to you a God; and you shall know that I am Yahweh your God, who brings you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will bring you into the land which I swore to give to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; and I will give it to you for a heritage: I am Yahweh.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses spoke so to the children of Israel, but they didn’t listen to Moses for anguish of spirit, and for cruel bondage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Go in, speak to Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses spoke before Yahweh, saying, “Behold, the children of Israel haven’t listened to me. How then shall Pharaoh listen to me, who am of uncircumcised lips?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, and gave them a command to the children of Israel, and to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are the heads of their fathers’ houses. The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, and Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi; these are the families of Reuben.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanite woman; these are the families of Simeon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations: Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari; and the years of the life of Levi were one hundred thirty-seven years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei, according to their families.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Kohath: Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel; and the years of the life of Kohath were one hundred thirty-three years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amram took Jochebed his father’s sister to himself as wife; and she bore him Aaron and Moses: and the years of the life of Amram were a hundred and thirty-seven years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Izhar: Korah, and Nepheg, and Zichri.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Uzziel: Mishael, and Elzaphan, and Sithri.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron took Elisheba, the daughter of Amminadab, the sister of Nahshon, as his wife; and she bore him Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Korah: Assir, and Elkanah, and Abiasaph; these are the families of the Korahites.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Eleazar Aaron’s son took one of the daughters of Putiel as his wife; and she bore him Phinehas. These are the heads of the fathers’ houses of the Levites according to their families.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are that Aaron and Moses, to whom Yahweh said, “Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their armies.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are those who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt. These are that Moses and Aaron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the day when Yahweh spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying, “I am Yahweh. Speak to Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I speak to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said before Yahweh, “Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips, and how shall Pharaoh listen to me?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Behold, I have made you as God to Pharaoh; and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall speak all that I command you; and Aaron your brother shall speak to Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Pharaoh will not listen to you, and I will lay my hand on Egypt, and bring out my armies, my people the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great judgments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Egyptians shall know that I am Yahweh, when I stretch out my hand on Egypt, and bring out the children of Israel from among them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron did so. As Yahweh commanded them, so they did.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty-three years old, when they spoke to Pharaoh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When Pharaoh speaks to you, saying, ‘Perform a miracle!’ then you shall tell Aaron, ‘Take your rod, and cast it down before Pharaoh, that it become a serpent.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh, and they did so, as Yahweh had commanded: and Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh and before his servants, and it became a serpent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Pharaoh also called for the wise men and the sorcerers. They also, the magicians of Egypt, did the same thing with their enchantments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they each cast down their rods, and they became serpents: but Aaron’s rod swallowed up their rods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he didn’t listen to them; as Yahweh had spoken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Pharaoh’s heart is stubborn. He refuses to let the people go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go to Pharaoh in the morning. Behold, he goes out to the water; and you shall stand by the river’s bank to meet him; and the rod which was turned to a serpent you shall take in your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall tell him, ‘Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, has sent me to you, saying, “Let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness:” and behold, until now you haven’t listened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh says, “In this you shall know that I am Yahweh. Behold, I will strike with the rod that is in my hand on the waters which are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fish that are in the river shall die, and the river shall become foul; and the Egyptians shall loathe to drink water from the river.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Tell Aaron, ‘Take your rod, and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt, over their rivers, over their streams, and over their pools, and over all their ponds of water, that they may become blood; and there shall be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron did so, as Yahweh commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and struck the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fish that were in the river died; and the river became foul, and the Egyptians couldn’t drink water from the river; and the blood was throughout all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The magicians of Egypt did the same thing with their enchantments; and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he didn’t listen to them; as Yahweh had spoken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and he didn’t even take this to heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the Egyptians dug around the river for water to drink; for they couldn’t drink the river water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seven days were fulfilled, after Yahweh had struck the river.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, Go in to Pharaoh, and tell him, “This is what Yahweh says, ‘Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you refuse to let them go, behold, I will plague all your borders with frogs:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the river shall swarm with frogs, which shall go up and come into your house, and into your bedroom, and on your bed, and into the house of your servants, and on your people, and into your ovens, and into your kneading troughs:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the frogs shall come up both on you, and on your people, and on all your servants.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Tell Aaron, ‘Stretch out your hand with your rod over the rivers, over the streams, and over the pools, and cause frogs to come up on the land of Egypt.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; and the frogs came up, and covered the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The magicians did the same thing with their enchantments, and brought up frogs on the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said, “Entreat Yahweh, that he take away the frogs from me, and from my people; and I will let the people go, that they may sacrifice to Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Pharaoh, “I give you the honor of setting the time that I should pray for you, and for your servants, and for your people, that the frogs be destroyed from you and your houses, and remain in the river only.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Tomorrow.” He said, “Be it according to your word, that you may know that there is no one like Yahweh our God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The frogs shall depart from you, and from your houses, and from your servants, and from your people. They shall remain in the river only.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh, and Moses cried to Yahweh concerning the frogs which he had brought on Pharaoh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh did according to the word of Moses, and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the courts, and out of the fields.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They gathered them together in heaps, and the land stank.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when Pharaoh saw that there was a respite, he hardened his heart, and didn’t listen to them, as Yahweh had spoken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Tell Aaron, ‘Stretch out your rod, and strike the dust of the earth, that it may become lice throughout all the land of Egypt.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They did so; and Aaron stretched out his hand with his rod, and struck the dust of the earth, and there were lice on man, and on animal; all the dust of the earth became lice throughout all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The magicians tried with their enchantments to produce lice, but they couldn’t. There were lice on man, and on animal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the magicians said to Pharaoh, “This is God’s finger:” and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he didn’t listen to them; as Yahweh had spoken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh; behold, he comes out to the water; and tell him, ‘This is what Yahweh says, “Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Else, if you will not let my people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies on you, and on your servants, and on your people, and into your houses: and the houses of the Egyptians shall be full of swarms of flies, and also the ground whereon they are.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will set apart in that day the land of Goshen, in which my people dwell, that no swarms of flies shall be there; to the end you may know that I am Yahweh on the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will put a division between my people and your people: by tomorrow shall this sign be.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh did so; and there came grievous swarms of flies into the house of Pharaoh, and into his servants’ houses: and in all the land of Egypt the land was corrupted by reason of the swarms of flies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh called for Moses and for Aaron, and said, “Go, sacrifice to your God in the land!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “It isn’t appropriate to do so; for we shall sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians to Yahweh our God. Behold, shall we sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians before their eyes, and won’t they stone us?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We will go three days’ journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to Yahweh our God, as he shall command us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh said, “I will let you go, that you may sacrifice to Yahweh your God in the wilderness, only you shall not go very far away. Pray for me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “Behold, I go out from you, and I will pray to Yahweh that the swarms of flies may depart from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people, tomorrow; only don’t let Pharaoh deal deceitfully any more in not letting the people go to sacrifice to Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses went out from Pharaoh, and prayed to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh did according to the word of Moses, and he removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people. There remained not one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh hardened his heart this time also, and he didn’t let the people go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Yahweh said to Moses, “Go in to Pharaoh, and tell him, ‘This is what Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, says: “Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For if you refuse to let them go, and hold them still,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: behold, Yahweh’s hand is on your livestock which are in the field, on the horses, on the donkeys, on the camels, on the herds, and on the flocks with a very grievous pestilence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt; and nothing shall die of all that belongs to the children of Israel.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh appointed a set time, saying, “Tomorrow Yahweh shall do this thing in the land.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh did that thing on the next day; and all the livestock of Egypt died, but of the livestock of the children of Israel, not one died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh sent, and, behold, there was not so much as one of the livestock of the Israelites dead. But the heart of Pharaoh was stubborn, and he didn’t let the people go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses and to Aaron, “Take to you handfuls of ashes of the furnace, and let Moses sprinkle it toward the sky in the sight of Pharaoh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall become small dust over all the land of Egypt, and shall be a boil breaking out with boils on man and on animal, throughout all the land of Egypt.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took ashes of the furnace, and stood before Pharaoh; and Moses sprinkled it up toward the sky; and it became a boil breaking out with boils on man and on animal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The magicians couldn’t stand before Moses because of the boils; for the boils were on the magicians, and on all the Egyptians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he didn’t listen to them, as Yahweh had spoken to Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh, and tell him, ‘This is what Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, says: “Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For this time I will send all my plagues against your heart, against your officials, and against your people; that you may know that there is no one like me in all the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For now I would have stretched out my hand, and struck you and your people with pestilence, and you would have been cut off from the earth;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but indeed for this cause I have made you stand: to show you my power, and that my name may be declared throughout all the earth;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: as you still exalt yourself against my people, that you won’t let them go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, tomorrow about this time I will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as has not been in Egypt since the day it was founded even until now.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore command that all of your livestock and all that you have in the field be brought into shelter. Every man and animal that is found in the field, and isn’t brought home, the hail shall come down on them, and they shall die.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who feared Yahweh’s word among the servants of Pharaoh made their servants and their livestock flee into the houses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever didn’t respect Yahweh’s word left his servants and his livestock in the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward the sky, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt, on man, and on animal, and on every herb of the field, throughout the land of Egypt.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses stretched out his rod toward the heavens, and Yahweh sent thunder, hail, and lightning flashed down to the earth. Yahweh rained hail on the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So there was very severe hail, and lightning mixed with the hail, such as had not been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The hail struck throughout all the land of Egypt all that was in the field, both man and animal; and the hail struck every herb of the field, and broke every tree of the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel were, there was no hail.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh sent, and called for Moses and Aaron, and said to them, “I have sinned this time. Yahweh is righteous, and I and my people are wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pray to Yahweh; for there has been enough of mighty thunderings and hail. I will let you go, and you shall stay no longer.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to him, “As soon as I have gone out of the city, I will spread abroad my hands to Yahweh. The thunders shall cease, and there will not be any more hail; that you may know that the earth is Yahweh’s.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for you and your servants, I know that you don’t yet fear Yahweh God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The flax and the barley were struck, for the barley was in the ear, and the flax was in bloom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the wheat and the spelt were not struck, for they had not grown up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses went out of the city from Pharaoh, and spread abroad his hands to Yahweh; and the thunders and hail ceased, and the rain was not poured on the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Pharaoh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunders had ceased, he sinned yet more, and hardened his heart, he and his servants.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he didn’t let the children of Israel go, just as Yahweh had spoken through Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Go in to Pharaoh, for I have hardened his heart, and the heart of his servants, that I may show these my signs among them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and that you may tell in the hearing of your son, and of your son’s son, what things I have done to Egypt, and my signs which I have done among them; that you may know that I am Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh, and said to him, “This is what Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, says: ‘How long will you refuse to humble yourself before me? Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or else, if you refuse to let my people go, behold, tomorrow I will bring locusts into your country,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they shall cover the surface of the earth, so that one won’t be able to see the earth. They shall eat the residue of that which has escaped, which remains to you from the hail, and shall eat every tree which grows for you out of the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your houses shall be filled, and the houses of all your servants, and the houses of all the Egyptians; as neither your fathers nor your fathers’ fathers have seen, since the day that they were on the earth to this day.’” He turned, and went out from Pharaoh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh’s servants said to him, “How long will this man be a snare to us? Let the men go, that they may serve Yahweh, their God. Don’t you yet know that Egypt is destroyed?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron were brought again to Pharaoh, and he said to them, “Go, serve Yahweh your God; but who are those who will go?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “We will go with our young and with our old; with our sons and with our daughters, with our flocks and with our herds will we go; for we must hold a feast to Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Yahweh be with you if I will let you go with your little ones! See, evil is clearly before your faces.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Not so! Go now you who are men, and serve Yahweh; for that is what you desire!” They were driven out from Pharaoh’s presence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, that they may come up on the land of Egypt, and eat every herb of the land, even all that the hail has left.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses stretched out his rod over the land of Egypt, and Yahweh brought an east wind on the land all that day, and all night; and when it was morning, the east wind brought the locusts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The locusts went up over all the land of Egypt, and rested in all the borders of Egypt. They were very grievous. Before them there were no such locusts as they, nor will there ever be again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they covered the surface of the whole earth, so that the land was darkened, and they ate every herb of the land, and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left. There remained nothing green, either tree or herb of the field, through all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron in haste, and he said, “I have sinned against Yahweh your God, and against you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore please forgive my sin again, and pray to Yahweh your God, that he may also take away from me this death.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went out from Pharaoh, and prayed to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh turned an exceeding strong west wind, which took up the locusts, and drove them into the Red Sea.  There remained not one locust in all the borders of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Yahweh hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he didn’t let the children of Israel go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward the sky, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, even darkness which may be felt.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses stretched out his hand toward the sky, and there was a thick darkness in all the land of Egypt for three days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They didn’t see one another, and nobody rose from his place for three days; but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh called to Moses, and said, “Go, serve Yahweh. Only let your flocks and your herds stay behind. Let your little ones also go with you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “You must also give into our hand sacrifices and burnt offerings, that we may sacrifice to Yahweh our God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Our livestock also shall go with us. Not a hoof shall be left behind, for of it we must take to serve Yahweh our God; and we don’t know with what we must serve Yahweh, until we come there.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Yahweh hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he wouldn’t let them go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh said to him, “Get away from me! Be careful to see my face no more; for in the day you see my face you shall die!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “You have spoken well. I will see your face again no more.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Yet one plague more will I bring on Pharaoh, and on Egypt; afterwards he will let you go. When he lets you go, he will surely thrust you out altogether.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Speak now in the ears of the people, and let every man ask of his neighbor, and every woman of her neighbor, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh’s servants, and in the sight of the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “This is what Yahweh says: ‘About midnight I will go out into the middle of Egypt,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne, even to the firstborn of the female servant who is behind the mill; and all the firstborn of livestock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as there has not been, nor shall be any more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But against any of the children of Israel a dog won’t even bark or move its tongue, against man or animal; that you may know that Yahweh makes a distinction between the Egyptians and Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All these servants of yours will come down to me, and bow down themselves to me, saying, “Get out, with all the people who follow you”; and after that I will go out.’” He went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Pharaoh won’t listen to you, that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh, and Yahweh hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he didn’t let the children of Israel go out of his land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “This month shall be to you the beginning of months. It shall be the first month of the year to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Speak to all the congregation of Israel, saying, ‘On the tenth day of this month, they shall take to them every man a lamb, according to their fathers’ houses, a lamb for a household;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and if the household is too little for a lamb, then he and his neighbor next to his house shall take one according to the number of the souls; according to what everyone can eat you shall make your count for the lamb.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your lamb shall be without defect, a male a year old. You shall take it from the sheep, or from the goats:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you shall keep it until the fourteenth day of the same month; and the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it at evening.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall take some of the blood, and put it on the two door posts and on the lintel, on the houses in which they shall eat it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall eat the meat in that night, roasted with fire, and unleavened bread. They shall eat it with bitter herbs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t eat it raw, nor boiled at all with water, but roasted with fire; with its head, its legs and its inner parts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall let nothing of it remain until the morning; but that which remains of it until the morning you shall burn with fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is how you shall eat it: with your belt on your waist, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and you shall eat it in haste: it is Yahweh’s Passover.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I will go through the land of Egypt in that night, and will strike all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and animal. Against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments: I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The blood shall be to you for a token on the houses where you are: and when I see the blood, I will pass over you, and there shall no plague be on you to destroy you, when I strike the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This day shall be to you for a memorial, and you shall keep it a feast to Yahweh: throughout your generations you shall keep it a feast by an ordinance forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread; even the first day you shall put away yeast out of your houses, for whoever eats leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the first day there shall be to you a holy convocation, and in the seventh day a holy convocation; no kind of work shall be done in them, except that which every man must eat, that only may be done by you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall observe the feast of unleavened bread; for in this same day I have brought your armies out of the land of Egypt: therefore you shall observe this day throughout your generations by an ordinance forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at evening, you shall eat unleavened bread, until the twenty first day of the month at evening.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall be no yeast found in your houses for seven days, for whoever eats that which is leavened, that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he is a foreigner, or one who is born in the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall eat nothing leavened. In all your habitations you shall eat unleavened bread.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel, and said to them, “Draw out, and take lambs according to your families, and kill the Passover.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take a bunch of hyssop, and dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and strike the lintel and the two door posts with the blood that is in the basin; and none of you shall go out of the door of his house until the morning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh will pass through to strike the Egyptians; and when he sees the blood on the lintel, and on the two door posts, Yahweh will pass over the door, and will not allow the destroyer to come in to your houses to strike you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall observe this thing for an ordinance to you and to your sons forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall happen when you have come to the land which Yahweh will give you, according as he has promised, that you shall keep this service.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It will happen, when your children ask you, ‘What do you mean by this service?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you shall say, ‘It is the sacrifice of Yahweh’s Passover, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt, when he struck the Egyptians, and spared our houses.’” The people bowed their heads and worshiped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel went and did so; as Yahweh had commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At midnight, Yahweh struck all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the firstborn of the captive who was in the dungeon; and all the firstborn of livestock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt, for there was not a house where there was not one dead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called for Moses and Aaron by night, and said, “Rise up, get out from among my people, both you and the children of Israel; and go, serve Yahweh, as you have said!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take both your flocks and your herds, as you have said, and be gone; and bless me also!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Egyptians were urgent with the people, to send them out of the land in haste, for they said, “We are all dead men.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneading troughs being bound up in their clothes on their shoulders.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel did according to the word of Moses; and they asked of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and clothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they let them have what they asked. They plundered the Egyptians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel traveled from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand on foot who were men, besides children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A mixed multitude went up also with them, with flocks, herds, and even very much livestock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought out of Egypt; for it wasn’t leavened, because they were thrust out of Egypt, and couldn’t wait, and they had not prepared any food for themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the time that the children of Israel lived in Egypt was four hundred thirty years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the end of four hundred thirty years, to the day, all of Yahweh’s armies went out from the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is a night to be much observed to Yahweh for bringing them out from the land of Egypt. This is that night of Yahweh, to be much observed of all the children of Israel throughout their generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses and Aaron, “This is the ordinance of the Passover. No foreigner shall eat of it,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but every man’s servant who is bought for money, when you have circumcised him, then shall he eat of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A foreigner and a hired servant shall not eat of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It must be eaten In one house. You shall not carry any of the meat outside of the house. Do not break any of its bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When a stranger shall live as a foreigner with you, and will keep the Passover to Yahweh, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one who is born in the land: but no uncircumcised person shall eat of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One law shall be to him who is born at home, and to the stranger who lives as a foreigner among you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the children of Israel did so. As Yahweh commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That same day, Yahweh brought the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their armies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Sanctify to me all the firstborn, whatever opens the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of animal. It is mine.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to the people, “Remember this day, in which you came out of Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand Yahweh brought you out from this place. No leavened bread shall be eaten.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Today you go out in the month Abib.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be, when Yahweh shall bring you into the land of the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, which he swore to your fathers to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, that you shall keep this service in this month.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Unleavened bread shall be eaten throughout the seven days; and no leavened bread shall be seen with you. No yeast shall be seen with you, within all your borders.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall tell your son in that day, saying, ‘It is because of that which Yahweh did for me when I came out of Egypt.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be for a sign to you on your hand, and for a memorial between your eyes, that Yahweh’s law may be in your mouth; for with a strong hand Yahweh has brought you out of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall therefore keep this ordinance in its season from year to year.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “It shall be, when Yahweh shall bring you into the land of the Canaanite, as he swore to you and to your fathers, and shall give it you,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you shall set apart to Yahweh all that opens the womb, and every firstborn which you have that comes from an animal. The males shall be Yahweh’s.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb; and if you will not redeem it, then you shall break its neck; and you shall redeem all the firstborn of man among your sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be, when your son asks you in time to come, saying, ‘What is this?’ that you shall tell him, ‘By strength of hand Yahweh brought us out from Egypt, from the house of bondage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, Yahweh killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man, and the firstborn of animal. Therefore I sacrifice to Yahweh all that opens the womb, being males; but all the firstborn of my sons I redeem.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be for a sign on your hand, and for symbols between your eyes: for by strength of hand Yahweh brought us out of Egypt.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Pharaoh had let the people go, God didn’t lead them by the way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God said, “Lest perhaps the people change their minds when they see war, and they return to Egypt”;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but God led the people around by the way of the wilderness by the Red Sea; and the children of Israel went up armed out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses took the bones of Joseph with him, for he had made the children of Israel swear, saying, “God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones away from here with you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took their journey from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, in the edge of the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh went before them by day in a pillar of cloud, to lead them on their way, and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light, that they might go by day and by night:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the pillar of cloud by day, and the pillar of fire by night, didn’t depart from before the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Speak to the children of Israel, that they turn back and encamp before Pihahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, before Baal Zephon. You shall encamp opposite it by the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, ‘They are entangled in the land. The wilderness has shut them in.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and he will follow after them; and I will get honor over Pharaoh, and over all his armies; and the Egyptians shall know that I am Yahweh.” They did so.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Egypt was told that the people had fled; and the heart of Pharaoh and of his servants was changed towards the people, and they said, “What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He prepared his chariot, and took his army with him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over all of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued the children of Israel; for the children of Israel went out with a high hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Egyptians pursued them. All the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, his horsemen, and his army overtook them encamping by the sea, beside Pihahiroth, before Baal Zephon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Pharaoh came near, the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and behold, the Egyptians were marching after them; and they were very afraid. The children of Israel cried out to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to Moses, “Because there were no graves in Egypt, have you taken us away to die in the wilderness? Why have you treated us this way, to bring us out of Egypt?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Isn’t this the word that we spoke to you in Egypt, saying, ‘Leave us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians?’ For it would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to the people, “Don’t be afraid. Stand still, and see the salvation of Yahweh, which he will work for you today: for the Egyptians whom you have seen today, you shall never see them again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will fight for you, and you shall be still.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Why do you cry to me? Speak to the children of Israel, that they go forward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lift up your rod, and stretch out your hand over the sea, and divide it: and the children of Israel shall go into the middle of the sea on dry ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I myself will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall go in after them: and I will get myself honor over Pharaoh, and over all his armies, over his chariots, and over his horsemen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Egyptians shall know that I am Yahweh, when I have gotten myself honor over Pharaoh, over his chariots, and over his horsemen.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The angel of God, who went before the camp of Israel, moved and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud moved from before them, and stood behind them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It came between the camp of Egypt and the camp of Israel; and there was the cloud and the darkness, yet gave it light by night: and one didn’t come near the other all night.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and Yahweh caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel went into the middle of the sea on the dry ground, and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand, and on their left.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Egyptians pursued, and went in after them into the middle of the sea: all of Pharaoh’s horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the morning watch, Yahweh looked out on the Egyptian army through the pillar of fire and of cloud, and confused the Egyptian army.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took off their chariot wheels, and they drove them heavily; so that the Egyptians said, “Let’s flee from the face of Israel, for Yahweh fights for them against the Egyptians!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the sea, that the waters may come again on the Egyptians, on their chariots, and on their horsemen.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to its strength when the morning appeared; and the Egyptians fled against it. Yahweh overthrew the Egyptians in the middle of the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The waters returned, and covered the chariots and the horsemen, even all Pharaoh’s army that went in after them into the sea. There remained not so much as one of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the children of Israel walked on dry land in the middle of the sea, and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand, and on their left.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus Yahweh saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the seashore.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Israel saw the great work which Yahweh did to the Egyptians, and the people feared Yahweh; and they believed in Yahweh, and in his servant Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Moses and the children of Israel sang this song to Yahweh, and said, “I will sing to Yahweh, for he has triumphed gloriously. The horse and his rider he has thrown into the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yah is my strength and song. He has become my salvation. This is my God, and I will praise him; my father’s God, and I will exalt him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh is a man of war. Yahweh is his name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has cast Pharaoh’s chariots and his army into the sea. His chosen captains are sunk in the Red Sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The deeps cover them. They went down into the depths like a stone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your right hand, Yahweh, is glorious in power. Your right hand, Yahweh, dashes the enemy in pieces.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the greatness of your excellency, you overthrow those who rise up against you. You send out your wrath. It consumes them as stubble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With the blast of your nostrils, the waters were piled up. The floods stood upright as a heap. The deeps were congealed in the heart of the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The enemy said, ‘I will pursue. I will overtake. I will divide the plunder. My desire shall be satisfied on them. I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You blew with your wind. The sea covered them. They sank like lead in the mighty waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who is like you, Yahweh, among the gods? Who is like you, glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You stretched out your right hand. The earth swallowed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You, in your loving kindness, have led the people that you have redeemed. You have guided them in your strength to your holy habitation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The peoples have heard. They tremble. Pangs have taken hold on the inhabitants of Philistia.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the chiefs of Edom were dismayed. Trembling takes hold of the mighty men of Moab. All the inhabitants of Canaan have melted away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Terror and dread falls on them. By the greatness of your arm they are as still as a stone — until your people pass over, Yahweh, until the people pass over who you have purchased.

translations.name: World English Bible
