translations.text: You shall bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of your inheritance, the place, Yahweh, which you have made for yourself to dwell in; the sanctuary, Lord, which your hands have established.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh shall reign forever and ever.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the horses of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and Yahweh brought back the waters of the sea on them; but the children of Israel walked on dry land in the middle of the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a tambourine in her hand; and all the women went out after her with tambourines and with dances.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Miriam answered them, “Sing to Yahweh, for he has triumphed gloriously. The horse and his rider he has thrown into the sea.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses led Israel onward from the Red Sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came to Marah, they couldn’t drink from the waters of Marah, for they were bitter. Therefore its name was called Marah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people murmured against Moses, saying, “What shall we drink?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he cried to Yahweh. Yahweh showed him a tree, and he threw it into the waters, and the waters were made sweet. There he made a statute and an ordinance for them, and there he tested them;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he said, “If you will diligently listen to Yahweh your God’s voice, and will do that which is right in his eyes, and will pay attention to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of the diseases on you, which I have put on the Egyptians; for I am Yahweh who heals you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water, and seventy palm trees: and they encamped there by the waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came to the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron in the wilderness;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the children of Israel said to them, “We wish that we had died by Yahweh’s hand in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the meat pots, when we ate our fill of bread, for you have brought us out into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Yahweh said to Moses, “Behold, I will rain bread from the sky for you, and the people shall go out and gather a day’s portion every day, that I may test them, whether they will walk in my law, or not.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall come to pass on the sixth day, that they shall prepare that which they bring in, and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron said to all the children of Israel, “At evening, then you shall know that Yahweh has brought you out from the land of Egypt;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and in the morning, then you shall see Yahweh’s glory; because he hears your murmurings against Yahweh. Who are we, that you murmur against us?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “Now Yahweh shall give you meat to eat in the evening, and in the morning bread to satisfy you; because Yahweh hears your murmurings which you murmur against him. And who are we? Your murmurings are not against us, but against Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Aaron, “Tell all the congregation of the children of Israel, ‘Come near before Yahweh, for he has heard your murmurings.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As Aaron spoke to the whole congregation of the children of Israel, they looked toward the wilderness, and behold, Yahweh’s glory appeared in the cloud.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel. Speak to them, saying, ‘At evening you shall eat meat, and in the morning you shall be filled with bread: and you shall know that I am Yahweh your God.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the evening, quail came up and covered the camp; and in the morning the dew lay around the camp.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the dew that lay had gone, behold, on the surface of the wilderness was a small round thing, small as the frost on the ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the children of Israel saw it, they said to one another, “What is it?” For they didn’t know what it was. Moses said to them, “It is the bread which Yahweh has given you to eat.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the thing which Yahweh has commanded: “Gather of it everyone according to his eating; an omer  a head, according to the number of your persons, you shall take it, every man for those who are in his tent.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel did so, and gathered some more, some less.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they measured it with an omer, he who gathered much had nothing over, and he who gathered little had no lack. They gathered every man according to his eating.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to them, “Let no one leave of it until the morning.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Notwithstanding they didn’t listen to Moses, but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and became foul: and Moses was angry with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They gathered it morning by morning, everyone according to his eating. When the sun grew hot, it melted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the sixth day, they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for each one, and all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “This is that which Yahweh has spoken, ‘Tomorrow is a solemn rest, a holy Sabbath to Yahweh. Bake that which you want to bake, and boil that which you want to boil; and all that remains over lay up for yourselves to be kept until the morning.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They laid it up until the morning, as Moses asked, and it didn’t become foul, and there were no worms in it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “Eat that today, for today is a Sabbath to Yahweh. Today you shall not find it in the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Six days you shall gather it, but on the seventh day is the Sabbath. In it there shall be none.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the seventh day, some of the people went out to gather, and they found none.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “How long do you refuse to keep my commandments and my laws?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, because Yahweh has given you the Sabbath, therefore he gives you on the sixth day the bread of two days. Everyone stay in his place. Let no one go out of his place on the seventh day.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the people rested on the seventh day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The house of Israel called its name Manna,  and it was like coriander seed, white; and its taste was like wafers with honey.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “This is the thing which Yahweh has commanded, ‘Let an omer-full of it be kept throughout your generations, that they may see the bread with which I fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Aaron, “Take a pot, and put an omer-full of manna in it, and lay it up before Yahweh, to be kept throughout your generations.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As Yahweh commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel ate the manna forty years, until they came to an inhabited land. They ate the manna until they came to the borders of the land of Canaan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now an omer is one tenth of an ephah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the congregation of the children of Israel traveled from the wilderness of Sin, by their journeys, according to Yahweh’s commandment, and encamped in Rephidim; but there was no water for the people to drink.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore the people quarreled with Moses, and said, “Give us water to drink.” Moses said to them, “Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you test Yahweh?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people were thirsty for water there; and the people murmured against Moses, and said, “Why have you brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us, our children, and our livestock with thirst?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses cried to Yahweh, saying, “What shall I do with these people? They are almost ready to stone me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Walk on before the people, and take the elders of Israel with you, and take the rod in your hand with which you struck the Nile, and go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will stand before you there on the rock in Horeb. You shall strike the rock, and water will come out of it, that the people may drink.” Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called the name of the place Massah,  and Meribah,  because the children of Israel quarreled, and because they tested Yahweh, saying, “Is Yahweh among us, or not?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Amalek came and fought with Israel in Rephidim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Joshua, “Choose men for us, and go out, fight with Amalek. Tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with God’s rod in my hand.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Joshua did as Moses had told him, and fought with Amalek; and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Moses held up his hand, Israel prevailed. When he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Moses’ hands were heavy; and they took a stone, and put it under him, and he sat on it. Aaron and Hur held up his hands, the one on the one side, and the other on the other side. His hands were steady until sunset.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joshua defeated Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Write this for a memorial in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua: that I will utterly blot out the memory of Amalek from under the sky.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses built an altar, and called its name Yahweh our Banner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Yah has sworn: ‘Yahweh will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses’ father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people, how that Yahweh had brought Israel out of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, received Zipporah, Moses’ wife, after he had sent her away,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and her two sons. The name of one son was Gershom,  for Moses said, “I have lived as a foreigner in a foreign land”.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The name of the other was Eliezer,  for he said, “My father’s God was my help and delivered me from Pharaoh’s sword.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife to Moses into the wilderness where he was encamped, at the Mountain of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to Moses, “I, your father-in-law Jethro, have come to you with your wife, and her two sons with her.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and bowed and kissed him. They asked each other of their welfare, and they came into the tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses told his father-in-law all that Yahweh had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the hardships that had come on them on the way, and how Yahweh delivered them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which Yahweh had done to Israel, in that he had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jethro said, “Blessed be Yahweh, who has delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh; who has delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now I know that Yahweh is greater than all gods because of the thing in which they dealt arrogantly against them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God. Aaron came with all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’ father-in-law before God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the next day, Moses sat to judge the people, and the people stood around Moses from the morning to the evening.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Moses’ father-in-law saw all that he did to the people, he said, “What is this thing that you do for the people? Why do you sit alone, and all the people stand around you from morning to evening?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to his father-in-law, “Because the people come to me to inquire of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they have a matter, they come to me, and I judge between a man and his neighbor, and I make them know the statutes of God, and his laws.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses’ father-in-law said to him, “The thing that you do is not good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will surely wear away, both you, and this people that is with you; for the thing is too heavy for you. You are not able to perform it yourself alone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen now to my voice. I will give you counsel, and God be with you. You represent the people before God, and bring the causes to God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall teach them the statutes and the laws, and shall show them the way in which they must walk, and the work that they must do.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover you shall provide out of all the people able men which fear God: men of truth, hating unjust gain; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them judge the people at all times. It shall be that every great matter they shall bring to you, but every small matter they shall judge themselves. So shall it be easier for you, and they shall share the load with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you will do this thing, and God commands you so, then you will be able to endure, and all these people also will go to their place in peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Moses listened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They judged the people at all times. They brought the hard causes to Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went his way into his own land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the third month after the children of Israel had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that same day they came into the wilderness of Sinai.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had departed from Rephidim, and had come to the wilderness of Sinai, they encamped in the wilderness; and there Israel encamped before the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses went up to God, and Yahweh called to him out of the mountain, saying, “This is what you shall tell the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘You have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles’ wings, and brought you to myself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore, if you will indeed obey my voice, and keep my covenant, then you shall be my own possession from among all peoples; for all the earth is mine;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you shall be to me a kingdom of priests, and a holy nation.’ These are the words which you shall speak to the children of Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which Yahweh commanded him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people answered together, and said, “All that Yahweh has spoken we will do.” Moses reported the words of the people to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Behold, I come to you in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with you, and may also believe you forever.” Moses told the words of the people to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Go to the people, and sanctify them today and tomorrow, and let them wash their garments,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and be ready against the third day; for on the third day Yahweh will come down in the sight of all the people on Mount Sinai.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set bounds to the people all around, saying, ‘Be careful that you don’t go up onto the mountain, or touch its border. Whoever touches the mountain shall be surely put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No hand shall touch him, but he shall surely be stoned or shot through; whether it is animal or man, he shall not live.’ When the trumpet sounds long, they shall come up to the mountain.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses went down from the mountain to the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their clothes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the people, “Be ready by the third day. Don’t have sexual relations with a woman.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the third day, when it was morning, there were thunders and lightnings, and a thick cloud on the mountain, and the sound of an exceedingly loud trumpet; and all the people who were in the camp trembled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses led the people out of the camp to meet God; and they stood at the lower part of the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All of Mount Sinai smoked, because Yahweh descended on it in fire; and its smoke ascended like the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mountain quaked greatly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the sound of the trumpet grew louder and louder, Moses spoke, and God answered him by a voice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh came down on Mount Sinai, to the top of the mountain. Yahweh called Moses to the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Go down, warn the people, lest they break through to Yahweh to gaze, and many of them perish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the priests also, who come near to Yahweh, sanctify themselves, lest Yahweh break out on them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Yahweh, “The people can’t come up to Mount Sinai, for you warned us, saying, ‘Set bounds around the mountain, and sanctify it.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to him, “Go down! You shall bring Aaron up with you, but don’t let the priests and the people break through to come up to Yahweh, lest he break out against them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Moses went down to the people, and told them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God  spoke all these words, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I am Yahweh your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall have no other gods before me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not make for yourselves an idol, nor any image of anything that is in the heavens above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: you shall not bow yourself down to them, nor serve them, for I, Yahweh your God, am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children, on the third and on the fourth generation of those who hate me,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and showing loving kindness to thousands of those who love me and keep my commandments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not take the name of Yahweh your God in vain, for Yahweh will not hold him guiltless who takes his name in vain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall labor six days, and do all your work,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to Yahweh your God. You shall not do any work in it, you, nor your son, nor your daughter, your male servant, nor your female servant, nor your livestock, nor your stranger who is within your gates;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for in six days Yahweh made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day; therefore Yahweh blessed the Sabbath day, and made it holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land which Yahweh your God gives you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not murder.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not commit adultery.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not steal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not give false testimony against your neighbor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not covet your neighbor’s house. You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, nor his male servant, nor his female servant, nor his ox, nor his donkey, nor anything that is your neighbor’s.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people perceived the thunderings, the lightnings, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking. When the people saw it, they trembled, and stayed at a distance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to Moses, “Speak with us yourself, and we will listen; but don’t let God speak with us, lest we die.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to the people, “Don’t be afraid, for God has come to test you, and that his fear may be before you, that you won’t sin.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people stayed at a distance, and Moses came near to the thick darkness where God was.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “This is what you shall tell the children of Israel: ‘You yourselves have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall most certainly not make alongside of me gods of silver, or gods of gold for yourselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make an altar of earth for me, and shall sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your cattle. In every place where I record my name I will come to you and I will bless you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you make me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of cut stones; for if you lift up your tool on it, you have polluted it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall not go up by steps to my altar, that your nakedness may not be exposed to it.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now these are the ordinances which you shall set before them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If you buy a Hebrew servant, he shall serve six years and in the seventh he shall go out free without paying anything.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he comes in by himself, he shall go out by himself. If he is married, then his wife shall go out with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if the servant shall plainly say, ‘I love my master, my wife, and my children. I will not go out free;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then his master shall bring him to God, and shall bring him to the door or to the doorpost, and his master shall bore his ear through with an awl, and he shall serve him for ever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man sells his daughter to be a female servant, she shall not go out as the male servants do.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If she doesn’t please her master, who has married her to himself, then he shall let her be redeemed. He shall have no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has dealt deceitfully with her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he marries her to his son, he shall deal with her as a daughter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he takes another wife to himself, he shall not diminish her food, her clothing, and her marital rights.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he doesn’t do these three things for her, she may go free without paying any money.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “One who strikes a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but not if it is unintentional, but God allows it to happen: then I will appoint you a place where he shall flee.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a man schemes and comes presumptuously on his neighbor to kill him, you shall take him from my altar, that he may die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Anyone who attacks his father or his mother shall be surely put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Anyone who kidnaps someone and sells him, or if he is found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Anyone who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone, or with his fist, and he doesn’t die, but is confined to bed;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if he rises again and walks around with his staff, then he who struck him shall be cleared: only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall provide for his healing until he is thoroughly healed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man strikes his servant or his maid with a rod, and he dies under his hand, he shall surely be punished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Notwithstanding, if he gets up after a day or two, he shall not be punished, for he is his property.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If men fight and hurt a pregnant woman so that she gives birth prematurely, and yet no harm follows, he shall be surely fined as much as the woman’s husband demands and the judges allow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if any harm follows, then you must take life for life,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: burning for burning, wound for wound, and bruise for bruise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man strikes his servant’s eye, or his maid’s eye, and destroys it, he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he strikes out his male servant’s tooth, or his female servant’s tooth, he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a bull gores a man or a woman to death, the bull shall surely be stoned, and its meat shall not be eaten; but the owner of the bull shall not be held responsible.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if the bull had a habit of goring in the past, and it has been testified to its owner, and he has not kept it in, but it has killed a man or a woman, the bull shall be stoned, and its owner shall also be put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a ransom is laid on him, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatever is laid on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whether it has gored a son or has gored a daughter, according to this judgment it shall be done to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If the bull gores a male servant or a female servant, thirty shekels  of silver shall be given to their master, and the ox shall be stoned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man opens a pit, or if a man digs a pit and doesn’t cover it, and a bull or a donkey falls into it,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the owner of the pit shall make it good. He shall give money to its owner, and the dead animal shall be his.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If one man’s bull injures another’s, so that it dies, then they shall sell the live bull, and divide its price; and they shall also divide the dead animal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or if it is known that the bull was in the habit of goring in the past, and its owner has not kept it in, he shall surely pay bull for bull, and the dead animal shall be his own.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man steals an ox or a sheep, and kills it, or sells it; he shall pay five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If the thief is found breaking in, and is struck so that he dies, there shall be no guilt of bloodshed for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If the sun has risen on him, guilt of bloodshed shall be for him; he shall make restitution. If he has nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If the stolen property is found in his hand alive, whether it is ox, donkey, or sheep, he shall pay double.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man causes a field or vineyard to be eaten, and lets his animal loose, and it grazes in another man’s field, he shall make restitution from the best of his own field, and from the best of his own vineyard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If fire breaks out, and catches in thorns so that the shocks of grain, or the standing grain, or the field are consumed; he who kindled the fire shall surely make restitution.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man delivers to his neighbor money or stuff to keep, and it is stolen out of the man’s house; if the thief is found, he shall pay double.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If the thief isn’t found, then the master of the house shall come near to God, to find out if he hasn’t put his hand to his neighbor’s goods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For every matter of trespass, whether it is for ox, for donkey, for sheep, for clothing, or for any kind of lost thing, about which one says, ‘This is mine,’ the cause of both parties shall come before God. He whom God condemns shall pay double to his neighbor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man delivers to his neighbor a donkey, an ox, a sheep, or any animal to keep, and it dies or is injured, or driven away, no man seeing it;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the oath of Yahweh shall be between them both, whether he hasn’t put his hand to his neighbor’s goods; and its owner shall accept it, and he shall not make restitution.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if it is stolen from him, he shall make restitution to its owner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If it is torn in pieces, let him bring it for evidence. He shall not make good that which was torn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man borrows anything of his neighbor’s, and it is injured, or dies, its owner not being with it, he shall surely make restitution.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If its owner is with it, he shall not make it good. If it is a leased thing, it came for its lease.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man entices a virgin who isn’t pledged to be married, and lies with her, he shall surely pay a dowry for her to be his wife.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If her father utterly refuses to give her to him, he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not allow a sorceress to live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Whoever has sex with an animal shall surely be put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He who sacrifices to any god, except to Yahweh only, shall be utterly destroyed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not wrong an alien or oppress him, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not take advantage of any widow or fatherless child.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you take advantage of them at all, and they cry at all to me, I will surely hear their cry;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and my wrath will grow hot, and I will kill you with the sword; and your wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If you lend money to any of my people with you who is poor, you shall not be to him as a creditor. You shall not charge him interest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you take your neighbor’s garment as collateral, you shall restore it to him before the sun goes down,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for that is his only covering, it is his garment for his skin. What would he sleep in? It will happen, when he cries to me, that I will hear, for I am gracious.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not blaspheme God, nor curse a ruler of your people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not delay to offer from your harvest and from the outflow of your presses. “You shall give the firstborn of your sons to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall do likewise with your cattle and with your sheep. Seven days it shall be with its mother, then on the eighth day you shall give it to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall be holy men to me, therefore you shall not eat any meat that is torn by animals in the field. You shall cast it to the dogs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not spread a false report. Don’t join your hand with the wicked to be a malicious witness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not follow a crowd to do evil. You shall not testify in court to side with a multitude to pervert justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall not favor a poor man in his cause.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If you meet your enemy’s ox or his donkey going astray, you shall surely bring it back to him again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you see the donkey of him who hates you fallen down under his burden, don’t leave him, you shall surely help him with it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not deny justice to your poor people in their lawsuits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Keep far from a false charge, and don’t kill the innocent and righteous: for I will not justify the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall take no bribe, for a bribe blinds those who have sight and perverts the words of the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not oppress an alien, for you know the heart of an alien, since you were aliens in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For six years you shall sow your land, and shall gather in its increase,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow, that the poor of your people may eat; and what they leave the animal of the field shall eat. In the same way, you shall deal with your vineyard and with your olive grove.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Six days you shall do your work, and on the seventh day you shall rest, that your ox and your donkey may have rest, and the son of your servant, and the alien may be refreshed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Be careful to do all things that I have said to you; and don’t invoke the name of other gods or even let them be heard out of your mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall observe a feast to me three times a year.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall observe the feast of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month Abib (for in it you came out of Egypt), and no one shall appear before me empty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: And the feast of harvest, the first fruits of your labors, which you sow in the field; and the feast of ingathering, at the end of the year, when you gather in your labors out of the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread. The fat of my feast shall not remain all night until the morning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The first of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of Yahweh your God. “You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I send an angel before you, to keep you by the way, and to bring you into the place which I have prepared.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pay attention to him, and listen to his voice. Don’t provoke him, for he will not pardon your disobedience, for my name is in him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if you indeed listen to his voice, and do all that I speak, then I will be an enemy to your enemies, and an adversary to your adversaries.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my angel shall go before you, and bring you in to the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Canaanite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite; and I will cut them off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor follow their practices, but you shall utterly overthrow them and demolish their pillars.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall serve Yahweh your God, and he will bless your bread and your water, and I will take sickness away from among you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No one will miscarry or be barren in your land. I will fulfill the number of your days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will send my terror before you, and will confuse all the people to whom you come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will send the hornet before you, which will drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate, and the animals of the field multiply against you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Little by little I will drive them out from before you, until you have increased and inherit the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will set your border from the Red Sea even to the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the River; for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand, and you shall drive them out before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make no covenant with them, nor with their gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall not dwell in your land, lest they make you sin against me, for if you serve their gods, it will surely be a snare to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to Moses, “Come up to Yahweh, you, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship from a distance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses alone shall come near to Yahweh, but they shall not come near. The people shall not go up with him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses came and told the people all Yahweh’s words, and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice, and said, “All the words which Yahweh has spoken will we do.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses wrote all Yahweh’s words, and rose up early in the morning, and built an altar under the mountain, and twelve pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent young men of the children of Israel, who offered burnt offerings and sacrificed peace offerings of cattle to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses took half of the blood and put it in basins, and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the book of the covenant and read it in the hearing of the people, and they said, “All that Yahweh has spoken will we do, and be obedient.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said, “Look, this is the blood of the covenant, which Yahweh has made with you concerning all these words.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel went up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They saw the God of Israel. Under his feet was like a paved work of sapphire  stone, like the skies for clearness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He didn’t lay his hand on the nobles of the children of Israel. They saw God, and ate and drank.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Come up to me on the mountain, and stay here, and I will give you the stone tablets with the law and the commands that I have written, that you may teach them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses rose up with Joshua, his servant, and Moses went up onto God’s Mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the elders, “Wait here for us, until we come again to you. Behold, Aaron and Hur are with you. Whoever is involved in a dispute can go to them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses went up on the mountain, and the cloud covered the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s glory settled on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days. The seventh day he called to Moses out of the middle of the cloud.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The appearance of Yahweh’s glory was like devouring fire on the top of the mountain in the eyes of the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses entered into the middle of the cloud, and went up on the mountain; and Moses was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Speak to the children of Israel, that they take an offering for me. From everyone whose heart makes him willing you shall take my offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the offering which you shall take from them: gold, silver, brass,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats’ hair,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: rams’ skins dyed red, sea cow hides, acacia wood,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: oil for the light, spices for the anointing oil and for the sweet incense,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod and for the breastplate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell among them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: According to all that I show you, the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all of its furniture, even so you shall make it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “They shall make an ark of acacia wood. Its length shall be two and a half cubits,  its width a cubit and a half, and a cubit and a half its height.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall overlay it with pure gold. You shall overlay it inside and outside, and you shall make a gold molding around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in its four feet. Two rings shall be on the one side of it, and two rings on the other side of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry the ark.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The poles shall be in the rings of the ark. They shall not be taken from it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put the testimony which I shall give you into the ark.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold. Two and a half cubits shall be its length, and a cubit and a half its width.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make two cherubim of hammered gold. You shall make them at the two ends of the mercy seat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Make one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other end. You shall make the cherubim on its two ends of one piece with the mercy seat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The cherubim shall spread out their wings upward, covering the mercy seat with their wings, with their faces toward one another. The faces of the cherubim shall be toward the mercy seat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put the mercy seat on top of the ark, and in the ark you shall put the testimony that I will give you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There I will meet with you, and I will tell you from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim which are on the ark of the testimony, all that I command you for the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make a table of acacia wood. Two cubits shall be its length, and a cubit its width, and one and a half cubits its height.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall overlay it with pure gold, and make a gold molding around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make a rim of a hand width around it. You shall make a golden molding on its rim around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make four rings of gold for it, and put the rings in the four corners that are on its four feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the rings shall be close to the rim, for places for the poles to carry the table.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold, that the table may be carried with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make its dishes, its spoons, its ladles, and its bowls to pour out offerings with. You shall make them of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set bread of the presence on the table before me always.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make a lamp stand of pure gold. Of hammered work shall the lamp stand be made, even its base, its shaft, its cups, its buds, and its flowers, shall be of one piece with it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall be six branches going out of its sides: three branches of the lamp stand out of its one side, and three branches of the lamp stand out of its other side;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: three cups made like almond blossoms in one branch, a bud and a flower; and three cups made like almond blossoms in the other branch, a bud and a flower, so for the six branches going out of the lamp stand;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and in the lamp stand four cups made like almond blossoms, its buds and its flowers;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, for the six branches going out of the lamp stand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their buds and their branches shall be of one piece with it, all of it one beaten work of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make its lamps seven, and they shall light its lamps to give light to the space in front of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Its snuffers and its snuff dishes shall be of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be made of a talent  of pure gold, with all these accessories.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: See that you make them after their pattern, which has been shown to you on the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, with cherubim. The work of the skillful workman you shall make them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits,  and the width of each curtain four cubits: all the curtains shall have one measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Five curtains shall be coupled together to one another; and the other five curtains shall be coupled to one another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the one curtain from the edge in the coupling; and likewise you shall make in the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the second coupling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make fifty loops in the one curtain, and you shall make fifty loops in the edge of the curtain that is in the second coupling. The loops shall be opposite to one another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains to one another with the clasps: and the tabernacle shall be a unit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make curtains of goats’ hair for a covering over the tabernacle. You shall make them eleven curtains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits: the eleven curtains shall have one measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shall double over the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outermost in the coupling, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which is outermost in the second coupling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make fifty clasps of brass, and put the clasps into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The overhanging part that remains of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side, of that which remains in the length of the curtains of the tent, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering of sea cow hides above.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and one and a half cubits the width of each board.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall be two tenons in each board, joined to one another: thus you shall make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards for the south side southward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for its two tenons, and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, twenty boards,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the far part of the tabernacle westward you shall make six boards.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle in the far part.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall be double beneath, and in the same way they shall be whole to its top to one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make bars of acacia wood: five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the far part westward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The middle bar in the middle of the boards shall pass through from end to end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold for places for the bars: and you shall overlay the bars with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set up the tabernacle according to the way that it was shown to you on the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make a veil of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, with cherubim. The work of the skillful workman shall it be made.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, on four sockets of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall hang up the veil under the clasps, and shall bring the ark of the testimony in there within the veil: and the veil shall separate the holy place from the most holy for you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set the table outside the veil, and the lamp stand over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and you shall put the table on the north side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make a screen for the door of the Tent, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the embroiderer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold: their hooks shall be of gold: and you shall cast five sockets of brass for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits  long, and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square: and its height shall be three cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it; and you shall overlay it with brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make its pots to take away its ashes, its shovels, its basins, its meat hooks, and its fire pans: all its vessels you shall make of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make a grating for it of network of brass: and on the net you shall make four bronze rings in its four corners.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put it under the ledge around the altar beneath, that the net may reach halfway up the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Its poles shall be put into the rings, and the poles shall be on the two sides of the altar, when carrying it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make it with hollow planks. They shall make it as it has been shown you on the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward there shall be hangings for the court of fine twined linen one hundred cubits long for one side:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and its pillars shall be twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets shall be of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Likewise for the north side in length there shall be hangings one hundred cubits long, and its pillars twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars, and their fillets, of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the width of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits; their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The width of the court on the east side eastward shall be fifty cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The hangings for the one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the other side shall be hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the gate of the court shall be a screen of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the embroiderer; their pillars four, and their sockets four.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the pillars of the court around shall be filleted with silver; their hooks of silver, and their sockets of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits, and the width fifty every where, and the height five cubits, of fine twined linen, and their sockets of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the instruments of the tabernacle in all its service, and all its pins, and all the pins of the court, shall be of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall command the children of Israel, that they bring to you pure olive oil beaten for the light, to cause a lamp to burn continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the Tent of Meeting, outside the veil which is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall keep it in order from evening to morning before Yahweh: it shall be a statute forever throughout their generations on the behalf of the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Bring Aaron your brother, and his sons with him, near to you from among the children of Israel, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office, even Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, for glory and for beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall speak to all who are wise-hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they make Aaron’s garments to sanctify him, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a coat of checker work, a turban, and a sash: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, and his sons, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall take the gold, and the blue, and the purple, and the scarlet, and the fine linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “They shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the skillful workman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall have two shoulder straps joined to the two ends of it, that it may be joined together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The skillfully woven band, which is on it, that is on him, shall be like its work and of the same piece; of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the children of Israel:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the six that remain on the other stone, in the order of their birth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With the work of an engraver in stone, like the engravings of a signet, you shall engrave the two stones, according to the names of the children of Israel: you shall make them to be enclosed in settings of gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put the two stones on the shoulder straps of the ephod, to be stones of memorial for the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before Yahweh on his two shoulders for a memorial.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make settings of gold,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and two chains of pure gold; you shall make them like cords of braided work: and you shall put the braided chains on the settings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make a breastplate of judgment, the work of the skillful workman; like the work of the ephod you shall make it; of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, you shall make it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be square and folded double; a span  shall be its length of it, and a span its width.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set in it settings of stones, four rows of stones: a row of ruby, topaz, and beryl shall be the first row;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the second row a turquoise, a sapphire,  and an emerald;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the fourth row a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper: they shall be enclosed in gold in their settings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The stones shall be according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names; like the engravings of a signet, everyone according to his name, they shall be for the twelve tribes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make on the breastplate chains like cords, of braided work of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make on the breastplate two rings of gold, and shall put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put the two braided chains of gold in the two rings at the ends of the breastplate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The other two ends of the two braided chains you shall put on the two settings, and put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod in its forepart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make two rings of gold, and you shall put them on the two ends of the breastplate, on its edge, which is toward the side of the ephod inward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make two rings of gold, and shall put them on the two shoulder straps of the ephod underneath, in its forepart, close by its coupling, above the skillfully woven band of the ephod.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall bind the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it may be on the skillfully woven band of the ephod, and that the breastplate may not swing out from the ephod.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment on his heart, when he goes in to the holy place, for a memorial before Yahweh continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be on Aaron’s heart, when he goes in before Yahweh: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel on his heart before Yahweh continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall have a hole for the head in the middle of it. It shall have a binding of woven work around its hole, as it were the hole of a coat of mail, that it not be torn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On its hem you shall make pomegranates of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, around its hem; and bells of gold between and around them:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, around the hem of the robe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be on Aaron to minister: and its sound shall be heard when he goes in to the holy place before Yahweh, and when he comes out, that he not die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make a plate of pure gold, and engrave on it, like the engravings of a signet, ‘HOLY TO YAHWEH.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put it on a lace of blue, and it shall be on the sash; on the front of the sash it shall be.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be on Aaron’s forehead, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall make holy in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always on his forehead, that they may be accepted before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall weave the coat in checker work of fine linen, and you shall make a turban of fine linen, and you shall make a sash, the work of the embroiderer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make coats for Aaron’s sons, and you shall make sashes for them and you shall make headbands for them, for glory and for beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put them on Aaron your brother, and on his sons with him, and shall anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister to me in the priest’s office.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make them linen breeches to cover the flesh of their nakedness; from the waist even to the thighs they shall reach:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall be on Aaron, and on his sons, when they go in to the Tent of Meeting, or when they come near to the altar to minister in the holy place; that they don’t bear iniquity, and die: it shall be a statute forever to him and to his offspring after him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “This is the thing that you shall do to them to make them holy, to minister to me in the priest’s office: take one young bull and two rams without defect,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil: you shall make them of fine wheat flour.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put them into one basket, and bring them in the basket, with the bull and the two rams.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the Tent of Meeting, and shall wash them with water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take the garments, and put on Aaron the coat, the robe of the ephod, the ephod, and the breastplate, and clothe him with the skillfully woven band of the ephod;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you shall set the turban on his head, and put the holy crown on the turban.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you shall take the anointing oil, and pour it on his head, and anoint him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall bring his sons, and put coats on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall clothe them with belts, Aaron and his sons, and bind headbands on them: and they shall have the priesthood by a perpetual statute: and you shall consecrate Aaron and his sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall bring the bull before the Tent of Meeting: and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall kill the bull before Yahweh, at the door of the Tent of Meeting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take of the blood of the bull, and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger; and you shall pour out all the blood at the base of the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take all the fat that covers the innards, the cover of the liver, the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, and burn them on the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the meat of the bull, and its skin, and its dung, you shall burn with fire outside of the camp: it is a sin offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall also take the one ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall kill the ram, and you shall take its blood, and sprinkle it around on the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall cut the ram into its pieces, and wash its innards, and its legs, and put them with its pieces, and with its head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall burn the whole ram on the altar: it is a burnt offering to Yahweh; it is a pleasant aroma, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you shall kill the ram, and take some of its blood, and put it on the tip of the right ear of Aaron, and on the tip of the right ear of his sons, and on the thumb of their right hand, and on the big toe of their right foot, and sprinkle the blood around on the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take of the blood that is on the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it on Aaron, and on his garments, and on his sons, and on the garments of his sons with him: and he shall be made holy, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons’ garments with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also you shall take some of the ram’s fat, the fat tail, the fat that covers the innards, the cover of the liver, the two kidneys, the fat that is on them, and the right thigh (for it is a ram of consecration),

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and one loaf of bread, one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of unleavened bread that is before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put all of this in Aaron’s hands, and in his sons’ hands, and shall wave them for a wave offering before Yahweh.
