translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take them from their hands, and burn them on the altar on the burnt offering, for a pleasant aroma before Yahweh: it is an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall take the breast of Aaron’s ram of consecration, and wave it for a wave offering before Yahweh: and it shall be your portion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the thigh of the wave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of consecration, even of that which is for Aaron, and of that which is for his sons:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and it shall be for Aaron and his sons as their portion forever from the children of Israel; for it is a wave offering: and it shall be a wave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifices of their peace offerings, even their wave offering to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him, to be anointed in them, and to be consecrated in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seven days shall the son who is priest in his place put them on, when he comes into the Tent of Meeting to minister in the holy place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall take the ram of consecration, and boil its meat in a holy place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron and his sons shall eat the meat of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, at the door of the Tent of Meeting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall eat those things with which atonement was made, to consecrate and sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat of it, because they are holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If anything of the meat of the consecration, or of the bread, remains to the morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall do so to Aaron, and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded you. You shall consecrate them seven days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every day you shall offer the bull of sin offering for atonement: and you shall cleanse the altar, when you make atonement for it; and you shall anoint it, to sanctify it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar, and sanctify it: and the altar shall be most holy; whatever touches the altar shall be holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now this is that which you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old day by day continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The one lamb you shall offer in the morning; and the other lamb you shall offer at evening:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and with the one lamb a tenth part of an ephah  of fine flour mixed with the fourth part of a hin of beaten oil, and the fourth part of a hin of wine for a drink offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The other lamb you shall offer at evening, and shall do to it according to the meal offering of the morning, and according to its drink offering, for a pleasant aroma, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the Tent of Meeting before Yahweh, where I will meet with you, to speak there to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There I will meet with the children of Israel; and the place shall be sanctified by my glory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will sanctify the Tent of Meeting and the altar: Aaron also and his sons I will sanctify, to minister to me in the priest’s office.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall know that I am Yahweh their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, that I might dwell among them: I am Yahweh their God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make an altar to burn incense on. You shall make it of acacia wood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Its length shall be a cubit,  and its width a cubit. It shall be square, and its height shall be two cubits. Its horns shall be of one piece with it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top, its sides around it, and its horns; and you shall make a gold molding around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make two golden rings for it under its molding; on its two ribs, on its two sides you shall make them; and they shall be for places for poles with which to bear it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put it before the veil that is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron shall burn incense of sweet spices on it every morning. When he tends the lamps, he shall burn it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Aaron lights the lamps at evening, he shall burn it, a perpetual incense before Yahweh throughout your generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall offer no strange incense on it, nor burnt offering, nor meal offering; and you shall pour no drink offering on it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron shall make atonement on its horns once in the year; with the blood of the sin offering of atonement once in the year he shall make atonement for it throughout your generations. It is most holy to Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When you take a census of the children of Israel, according to those who are counted among them, then each man shall give a ransom for his soul to Yahweh, when you count them; that there be no plague among them when you count them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall give this, everyone who passes over to those who are counted, half a shekel after the shekel  of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs ); half a shekel for an offering to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone who passes over to those who are counted, from twenty years old and upward, shall give the offering to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less, than the half shekel,  when they give the offering of Yahweh, to make atonement for your souls.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall take the atonement money from the children of Israel, and shall appoint it for the service of the Tent of Meeting; that it may be a memorial for the children of Israel before Yahweh, to make atonement for your souls.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall also make a basin of brass, and its base of brass, in which to wash. You shall put it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and you shall put water in it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet in it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they go into the Tent of Meeting, they shall wash with water, that they not die; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they not die: and it shall be a statute forever to them, even to him and to his descendants throughout their generations.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Also take fine spices: of liquid myrrh, five hundred shekels;  and of fragrant cinnamon half as much, even two hundred and fifty; and of fragrant cane, two hundred and fifty;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and of cassia five hundred, after the shekel of the sanctuary; and a hin of olive oil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make it a holy anointing oil, a perfume compounded after the art of the perfumer: it shall be a holy anointing oil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall use it to anoint the Tent of Meeting, the ark of the testimony,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the table and all its articles, the lamp stand and its accessories, the altar of incense,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the basin with its base.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall sanctify them, that they may be most holy. Whatever touches them shall be holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall anoint Aaron and his sons, and sanctify them, that they may minister to me in the priest’s office.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall speak to the children of Israel, saying, ‘This shall be a holy anointing oil to me throughout your generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall not be poured on man’s flesh, and do not make any like it, according to its composition. It is holy. It shall be holy to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever compounds any like it, or whoever puts any of it on a stranger, he shall be cut off from his people.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Take to yourself sweet spices, gum resin, and onycha, and galbanum; sweet spices with pure frankincense: there shall be an equal weight of each;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you shall make incense of it, a perfume after the art of the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you shall beat some of it very small, and put some of it before the testimony in the Tent of Meeting, where I will meet with you. It shall be to you most holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The incense which you shall make, according to its composition you shall not make for yourselves: it shall be to you holy for Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever shall make any like that, to smell of it, he shall be cut off from his people.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I have called by name Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I have filled him with the Spirit of God, in wisdom, and in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all kinds of workmanship,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to devise skillful works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and in cutting of stones for setting, and in carving of wood, to work in all kinds of workmanship.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I myself have appointed with him Oholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan; and in the heart of all who are wise-hearted I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded you:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the Tent of Meeting, the ark of the testimony, the mercy seat that is on it, all the furniture of the Tent,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the table and its vessels, the pure lamp stand with all its vessels, the altar of incense,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the altar of burnt offering with all its vessels, the basin and its base,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the finely worked garments — the holy garments for Aaron the priest — the garments of his sons to minister in the priest’s office,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the anointing oil, and the incense of sweet spices for the holy place: according to all that I have commanded you they shall do.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Speak also to the children of Israel, saying, ‘Most certainly you shall keep my Sabbaths: for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that you may know that I am Yahweh who sanctifies you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall keep the Sabbath therefore; for it is holy to you. Everyone who profanes it shall surely be put to death; for whoever does any work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, holy to Yahweh. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day shall surely be put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore the children of Israel shall keep the Sabbath, to observe the Sabbath throughout their generations, for a perpetual covenant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is a sign between me and the children of Israel forever; for in six days Yahweh made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He gave to Moses, when he finished speaking with him on Mount Sinai, the two tablets of the testimony, stone tablets, written with God’s finger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the people saw that Moses delayed to come down from the mountain, the people gathered themselves together to Aaron, and said to him, “Come, make us gods, which shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what has become of him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron said to them, “Take off the golden rings, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring them to me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people took off the golden rings which were in their ears, and brought them to Aaron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He received what they handed him, and fashioned it with an engraving tool, and made it a molten calf; and they said, “These are your gods, Israel, which brought you up out of the land of Egypt.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it; and Aaron made a proclamation, and said, “Tomorrow shall be a feast to Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They rose up early on the next day, and offered burnt offerings, and brought peace offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to play.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, “Go, get down; for your people, who you brought up out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted themselves!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them. They have made themselves a molten calf, and have worshiped it, and have sacrificed to it, and said, ‘These are your gods, Israel, which brought you up out of the land of Egypt.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “I have seen these people, and behold, they are a stiff-necked people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore leave me alone, that my wrath may burn hot against them, and that I may consume them; and I will make of you a great nation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses begged Yahweh his God, and said, “Yahweh, why does your wrath burn hot against your people, that you have brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a mighty hand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why should the Egyptians speak, saying, ‘He brought them out for evil, to kill them in the mountains, and to consume them from the surface of the earth?’ Turn from your fierce wrath, and repent of this evil against your people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, your servants, to whom you swore by your own self, and said to them, ‘I will multiply your offspring  as the stars of the sky, and all this land that I have spoken of I will give to your offspring,  and they shall inherit it forever.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh repented of the evil which he said he would do to his people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses turned, and went down from the mountain, with the two tablets of the testimony in his hand; tablets that were written on both their sides; on the one side and on the other they were written.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved on the tablets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said to Moses, “There is the noise of war in the camp.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “It isn’t the voice of those who shout for victory. It is not the voice of those who cry for being overcome; but the noise of those who sing that I hear.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As soon as he came near to the camp, he saw the calf and the dancing. Then Moses’ anger grew hot, and he threw the tablets out of his hands, and broke them beneath the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the calf which they had made, and burned it with fire, ground it to powder, and scattered it on the water, and made the children of Israel drink of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Aaron, “What did these people do to you, that you have brought a great sin on them?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aaron said, “Don’t let the anger of my lord grow hot. You know the people, that they are set on evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they said to me, ‘Make us gods, which shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what has become of him.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said to them, ‘Whoever has any gold, let them take it off:’ so they gave it to me; and I threw it into the fire, and out came this calf.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Moses saw that the people had broken loose, (for Aaron had let them loose for a derision among their enemies),

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, “Whoever is on Yahweh’s side, come to me!” All the sons of Levi gathered themselves together to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Yahweh says, the God of Israel, ‘Every man put his sword on his thigh, and go back and forth from gate to gate throughout the camp, and every man kill his brother, and every man his companion, and every man his neighbor.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Levi did according to the word of Moses: and there fell of the people that day about three thousand men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said, “Consecrate yourselves today to Yahweh, yes, every man against his son, and against his brother; that he may give you a blessing today.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the next day, Moses said to the people, “You have sinned a great sin. Now I will go up to Yahweh. Perhaps I shall make atonement for your sin.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses returned to Yahweh, and said, “Oh, this people have sinned a great sin, and have made themselves gods of gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet now, if you will, forgive their sin — and if not, please blot me out of your book which you have written.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Whoever has sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now go, lead the people to the place of which I have spoken to you. Behold, my angel shall go before you. Nevertheless in the day when I punish, I will punish them for their sin.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh struck the people, because they made the calf, which Aaron made.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, “Depart, go up from here, you and the people that you have brought up out of the land of Egypt, to the land of which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, ‘I will give it to your offspring. ’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will send an angel before you; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to a land flowing with milk and honey: for I will not go up among you, for you are a stiff-necked people, lest I consume you on the way.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the people heard this evil news, they mourned: and no one put on his jewelry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Tell the children of Israel, ‘You are a stiff-necked people. If I were to go up into the middle of you for one moment, I would consume you. Therefore now take off your jewelry from you, that I may know what to do to you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel stripped themselves of their jewelry from Mount Horeb onward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Moses used to take the tent and to pitch it outside the camp, far away from the camp, and he called it “The Tent of Meeting.” Everyone who sought Yahweh went out to the Tent of Meeting, which was outside the camp.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Moses went out to the Tent, that all the people rose up, and stood, everyone at their tent door, and watched Moses, until he had gone into the Tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Moses entered into the Tent, the pillar of cloud descended, stood at the door of the Tent, and spoke with Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people saw the pillar of cloud stand at the door of the Tent, and all the people rose up and worshiped, everyone at their tent door.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. He turned again into the camp, but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, didn’t depart from the Tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to Yahweh, “Behold, you tell me, ‘Bring up this people:’ and you haven’t let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found favor in my sight.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore, if I have found favor in your sight, please show me now your way, that I may know you, so that I may find favor in your sight: and consider that this nation is your people.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “My presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “If your presence doesn’t go with me, don’t carry us up from here.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For how would people know that I have found favor in your sight, I and your people? Isn’t it that you go with us, so that we are separated, I and your people, from all the people who are on the surface of the earth?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “I will do this thing also that you have spoken; for you have found favor in my sight, and I know you by name.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Please show me your glory.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “I will make all my goodness pass before you, and will proclaim Yahweh’s name before you. I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will show mercy on whom I will show mercy.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “You cannot see my face, for man may not see me and live.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh also said, “Behold, there is a place by me, and you shall stand on the rock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It will happen, while my glory passes by, that I will put you in a cleft of the rock, and will cover you with my hand until I have passed by;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then I will take away my hand, and you will see my back; but my face shall not be seen.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Chisel two stone tablets like the first: and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets, which you broke.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be ready by the morning, and come up in the morning to Mount Sinai, and present yourself there to me on the top of the mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No one shall come up with you or be seen anywhere on the mountain. Do not let the flocks or herds graze in front of that mountain.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He chiseled two tablets of stone like the first; and Moses rose up early in the morning, and went up to Mount Sinai, as Yahweh had commanded him, and took in his hand two stone tablets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh descended in the cloud, and stood with him there, and proclaimed Yahweh’s name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh passed by before him, and proclaimed, “Yahweh! Yahweh, a merciful and gracious God, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness and truth,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: keeping loving kindness for thousands, forgiving iniquity and disobedience and sin; and that will by no means clear the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children, and on the children’s children, on the third and on the fourth generation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses hurried and bowed his head toward the earth, and worshiped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “If now I have found favor in your sight, Lord, please let the Lord go among us; although this is a stiff-necked people; pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your inheritance.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Behold, I make a covenant: before all your people I will do marvels, such as have not been worked in all the earth, nor in any nation; and all the people among which you are shall see the work of Yahweh; for it is an awesome thing that I do with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Observe that which I command you today. Behold, I drive out before you the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be careful, lest you make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land where you are going, lest it be for a snare in the middle of you:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but you shall break down their altars, and dash in pieces their pillars, and you shall cut down their Asherah poles;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for you shall worship no other god: for Yahweh, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Don’t make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, lest they play the prostitute after their gods, and sacrifice to their gods, and one call you and you eat of his sacrifice;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you take of their daughters to your sons, and their daughters play the prostitute after their gods, and make your sons play the prostitute after their gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall make no cast idols for yourselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall keep the feast of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month Abib; for in the month Abib you came out of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “All that opens the womb is mine; and all your livestock that is male, the firstborn of cow and sheep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a lamb. If you will not redeem it, then you shall break its neck. You shall redeem all the firstborn of your sons. No one shall appear before me empty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest: in plowing time and in harvest you shall rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall observe the feast of weeks with the first fruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of harvest at the year’s end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord Yahweh, the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I will drive out nations before you and enlarge your borders; neither shall any man desire your land when you go up to appear before Yahweh, your God, three times in the year.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread. The sacrifice of the feast of the Passover shall not be left to the morning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall bring the first of the first fruits of your ground to the house of Yahweh your God. “You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Write you these words: for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was there with Yahweh forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread, nor drank water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the testimony in Moses’ hand, when he came down from the mountain, Moses didn’t know that the skin of his face shone by reason of his speaking with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come near him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned to him; and Moses spoke to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Afterward all the children of Israel came near, and he gave them all the commandments that Yahweh had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Moses was done speaking with them, he put a veil on his face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when Moses went in before Yahweh to speak with him, he took the veil off, until he came out; and he came out, and spoke to the children of Israel that which he was commanded.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel saw Moses’ face, that the skin of Moses’ face shone: and Moses put the veil on his face again, until he went in to speak with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses assembled all the congregation of the children of Israel, and said to them, “These are the words which Yahweh has commanded, that you should do them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be a holy day for you, a Sabbath of solemn rest to Yahweh: whoever does any work in it shall be put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations on the Sabbath day.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses spoke to all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, “This is the thing which Yahweh commanded, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Take from among you an offering to Yahweh. Whoever is of a willing heart, let him bring it, Yahweh’s offering: gold, silver, brass,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats’ hair,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: rams’ skins dyed red, sea cow hides, acacia wood,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: oil for the light, spices for the anointing oil and for the sweet incense,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod and for the breastplate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘Let every wise-hearted man among you come, and make all that Yahweh has commanded:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the tabernacle, its outer covering, its roof, its clasps, its boards, its bars, its pillars, and its sockets;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the ark, and its poles, the mercy seat, the veil of the screen;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the table with its poles and all its vessels, and the show bread;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the lamp stand also for the light, with its vessels, its lamps, and the oil for the light;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the altar of incense with its poles, the anointing oil, the sweet incense, the screen for the door, at the door of the tabernacle;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the altar of burnt offering, with its grating of brass, it poles, and all its vessels, the basin and its base;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the hangings of the court, its pillars, their sockets, and the screen for the gate of the court;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the pins of the tabernacle, the pins of the court, and their cords;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the finely worked garments, for ministering in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest’s office.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came, everyone whose heart stirred him up, and everyone whom his spirit made willing, and brought Yahweh’s offering, for the work of the Tent of Meeting, and for all of its service, and for the holy garments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came, both men and women, as many as were willing-hearted, and brought brooches, earrings, signet rings, and armlets, all jewels of gold; even every man who offered an offering of gold to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone, with whom was found blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats’ hair, rams’ skins dyed red, and sea cow hides, brought them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone who offered an offering of silver and brass brought Yahweh’s offering; and everyone, with whom was found acacia wood for any work of the service, brought it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the women who were wise-hearted spun with their hands, and brought that which they had spun, the blue, the purple, the scarlet, and the fine linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the women whose heart stirred them up in wisdom spun the goats’ hair.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rulers brought the onyx stones, and the stones to be set, for the ephod and for the breastplate;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the spice, and the oil for the light, for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel brought a freewill offering to Yahweh; every man and woman, whose heart made them willing to bring for all the work, which Yahweh had commanded to be made by Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses said to the children of Israel, “Behold, Yahweh has called by name Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has filled him with the Spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, in knowledge, and in all kinds of workmanship;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and to make skillful works, to work in gold, in silver, in brass,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: in cutting of stones for setting, and in carving of wood, to work in all kinds of skillful workmanship.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has put in his heart that he may teach, both he, and Oholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has filled them with wisdom of heart, to work all kinds of workmanship, of the engraver, of the skillful workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen, and of the weaver, even of those who do any workmanship, and of those who make skillful works.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Bezalel and Oholiab shall work with every wise-hearted man, in whom Yahweh has put wisdom and understanding to know how to do all the work for the service of the sanctuary, according to all that Yahweh has commanded.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab, and every wise-hearted man, in whose heart Yahweh had put wisdom, even everyone whose heart stirred him up to come to the work to do it:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they received from Moses all the offering which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, with which to make it. They brought yet to him freewill offerings every morning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the wise men, who performed all the work of the sanctuary, each came from his work which they did.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They spoke to Moses, saying, “The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work which Yahweh commanded to make.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, “Let neither man nor woman make anything else for the offering for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the wise-hearted men among those who did the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twined linen, blue, purple, and scarlet, with cherubim, the work of the skillful workman, they made them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits,  and the width of each curtain four cubits. All the curtains had one measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He coupled five curtains to one another, and the other five curtains he coupled to one another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made loops of blue on the edge of the one curtain from the edge in the coupling. Likewise he made in the edge of the curtain that was outermost in the second coupling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made fifty loops in the one curtain, and he made fifty loops in the edge of the curtain that was in the second coupling. The loops were opposite to one another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made fifty clasps of gold, and coupled the curtains to one another with the clasps: so the tabernacle was a unit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made curtains of goats’ hair for a covering over the tabernacle. He made them eleven curtains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The length of each curtain was thirty cubits, and four cubits the width of each curtain. The eleven curtains had one measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He coupled five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that was outermost in the coupling, and he made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which was outermost in the second coupling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made fifty clasps of brass to couple the tent together, that it might be a unit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering of sea cow hides above.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ten cubits was the length of a board, and a cubit and a half the width of each board.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Each board had two tenons, joined to one another. He made all the boards of the tabernacle this way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side southward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for its two tenons, and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty boards,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the far part of the tabernacle westward he made six boards.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made two boards for the corners of the tabernacle in the far part.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were double beneath, and in the same way they were all the way to its top to one ring. He did this to both of them in the two corners.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; under every board two sockets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made bars of acacia wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the hinder part westward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the middle bar to pass through in the middle of the boards from the one end to the other.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings of gold for places for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the veil of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen: with cherubim. He made it the work of a skillful workman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made four pillars of acacia for it, and overlaid them with gold. Their hooks were of gold. He cast four sockets of silver for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made a screen for the door of the tent, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of an embroiderer;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the five pillars of it with their hooks. He overlaid their capitals and their fillets with gold, and their five sockets were of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood. Its length was two and a half cubits,  and its width a cubit and a half, and a cubit and a half its height.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made a molding of gold for it around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He cast four rings of gold for it, in its four feet; even two rings on its one side, and two rings on its other side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, to bear the ark.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made a mercy seat of pure gold. Its length was two and a half cubits, and a cubit and a half its width.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made two cherubim of gold. He made them of beaten work, at the two ends of the mercy seat;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other end. He made the cherubim of one piece with the mercy seat at its two ends.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The cherubim spread out their wings on high, covering the mercy seat with their wings, with their faces toward one another. The faces of the cherubim were toward the mercy seat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the table of acacia wood. Its length was two cubits, and its width was a cubit, and its height was a cubit and a half.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He overlaid it with pure gold, and made a gold molding around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made a border of a hand width around it, and made a golden molding on its border around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He cast four rings of gold for it, and put the rings in the four corners that were on its four feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rings were close by the border, the places for the poles to carry the table.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold, to carry the table.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the vessels which were on the table, its dishes, its spoons, its bowls, and its pitchers with which to pour out, of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the lamp stand of pure gold. He made the lamp stand of beaten work. Its base, its shaft, its cups, its buds, and its flowers were of one piece with it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were six branches going out of its sides: three branches of the lamp stand out of its one side, and three branches of the lamp stand out of its other side:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: three cups made like almond blossoms in one branch, a bud and a flower, and three cups made like almond blossoms in the other branch, a bud and a flower: so for the six branches going out of the lamp stand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the lamp stand were four cups made like almond blossoms, its buds and its flowers;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, for the six branches going out of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their buds and their branches were of one piece with it. The whole thing was one beaten work of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made its seven lamps, and its snuffers, and its snuff dishes, of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made it of a talent  of pure gold, with all its vessels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the altar of incense of acacia wood. It was square: its length was a cubit, and its width a cubit. Its height was two cubits. Its horns were of one piece with it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He overlaid it with pure gold, its top, its sides around it, and its horns. He made a gold molding around it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made two golden rings for it under its molding crown, on its two ribs, on its two sides, for places for poles with which to carry it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the holy anointing oil and the pure incense of sweet spices, after the art of the perfumer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood. It was square. Its length was five cubits,  its width was five cubits, and its height was three cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made its horns on its four corners. Its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, the shovels, the basins, the forks, and the fire pans. He made all its vessels of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made for the altar a grating of a network of brass, under the ledge around it beneath, reaching halfway up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He cast four rings for the four ends of brass grating, to be places for the poles.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar, with which to carry it. He made it hollow with planks.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the basin of brass, and its base of brass, out of the mirrors of the ministering women who ministered at the door of the Tent of Meeting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the court: for the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, one hundred cubits;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: their pillars were twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the north side one hundred cubits, their pillars twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars, and their fillets, of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the west side were hangings of fifty cubits, their pillars ten, and their sockets ten; the hooks of the pillars, and their fillets, of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the east side eastward fifty cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The hangings for the one side were fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and so for the other side: on this hand and that hand by the gate of the court were hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the hangings around the court were of fine twined linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sockets for the pillars were of brass. The hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver; and the overlaying of their capitals, of silver; and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The screen for the gate of the court was the work of the embroiderer, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen. Twenty cubits was the length, and the height in the width was five cubits, like to the hangings of the court.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their pillars were four, and their sockets four, of brass; their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals, and their fillets, of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the pins of the tabernacle, and around the court, were of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the amount of material used for the tabernacle, even the Tabernacle of the Testimony, as they were counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With him was Oholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a skillful workman, and an embroiderer in blue, in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the gold that was used for the work in all the work of the sanctuary, even the gold of the offering, was twenty-nine talents,  and seven hundred thirty shekels, after the shekel  of the sanctuary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The silver of those who were counted of the congregation was one hundred talents,  and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels,  after the shekel of the sanctuary:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a beka  a head, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel  of the sanctuary, for everyone who passed over to those who were counted, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The one hundred talents  of silver were for casting the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the veil; one hundred sockets for the one hundred talents, a talent for a socket.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Of the one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels  he made hooks for the pillars, overlaid their capitals, and made fillets for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The brass of the offering was seventy talents,  and two thousand four hundred shekels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With this he made the sockets to the door of the Tent of Meeting, the bronze altar, the bronze grating for it, all the vessels of the altar,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the sockets around the court, the sockets of the gate of the court, all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins around the court.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Of the blue, purple, and scarlet, they made finely worked garments, for ministering in the holy place, and made the holy garments for Aaron; as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They beat the gold into thin plates, and cut it into wires, to work it in the blue, in the purple, in the scarlet, and in the fine linen, the work of the skillful workman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made shoulder straps for it, joined together. At the two ends it was joined together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The skillfully woven band that was on it, with which to fasten it on, was of the same piece, like its work; of gold, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen; as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They worked the onyx stones, enclosed in settings of gold, engraved with the engravings of a signet, according to the names of the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod, to be stones of memorial for the children of Israel, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the breastplate, the work of a skillful workman, like the work of the ephod; of gold, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was square. They made the breastplate double. Its length was a span,  and its width a span, being double.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They set in it four rows of stones. A row of ruby, topaz, and beryl was the first row;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the second row, a turquoise, a sapphire,  and an emerald;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the third row, a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the fourth row, a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper. They were enclosed in gold settings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The stones were according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names; like the engravings of a signet, everyone according to his name, for the twelve tribes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made on the breastplate chains like cords, of braided work of pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made two settings of gold, and two gold rings, and put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They put the two braided chains of gold in the two rings at the ends of the breastplate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The other two ends of the two braided chains they put on the two settings, and put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod, in its front.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made two rings of gold, and put them on the two ends of the breastplate, on its edge, which was toward the side of the ephod inward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made two rings of gold, and put them on the two shoulder straps of the ephod underneath, in its front, close by its coupling, above the skillfully woven band of the ephod.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They bound the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it might be on the skillfully woven band of the ephod, and that the breastplate might not come loose from the ephod, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the robe of the ephod of woven work, all of blue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The opening of the robe in the middle of it was like the opening of a coat of mail, with a binding around its opening, that it should not be torn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made on the skirts of the robe pomegranates of blue, purple, scarlet, and twined linen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates around the skirts of the robe, between the pomegranates;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, around the skirts of the robe, to minister in, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made the coats of fine linen of woven work for Aaron, and for his sons,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the turban of fine linen, and the linen headbands of fine linen, and the linen breeches of fine twined linen,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the sash of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, the work of the embroiderer, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and wrote on it a writing, like the engravings of a signet: “HOLY TO YAHWEH”.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They tied to it a lace of blue, to fasten it on the turban above, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting was finished. The children of Israel did according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses; so they did.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought the tabernacle to Moses, the tent, with all its furniture, its clasps, its boards, its bars, its pillars, its sockets,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the covering of rams’ skins dyed red, the covering of sea cow hides, the veil of the screen,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the ark of the testimony with its poles, the mercy seat,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the table, all its vessels, the show bread,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the pure lamp stand, its lamps, even the lamps to be set in order, all its vessels, the oil for the light,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the golden altar, the anointing oil, the sweet incense, the screen for the door of the Tent,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the bronze altar, its grating of brass, its poles, all of its vessels, the basin and its base,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the hangings of the court, its pillars, its sockets, the screen for the gate of the court, its cords, its pins, all the instruments of the service of the tabernacle, for the Tent of Meeting,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the finely worked garments for ministering in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest’s office.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did all the work.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it as Yahweh had commanded, even so had they done it: and Moses blessed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “On the first day of the first month you shall raise up the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put the ark of the testimony in it, and you shall screen the ark with the veil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall bring in the table, and set in order the things that are on it. You shall bring in the lamp stand, and light its lamps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony, and put the screen of the door to the tabernacle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and shall put water therein.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall set up the court around it, and hang up the screen of the gate of the court.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle, and all that is in it, and shall make it holy, and all its furniture: and it will be holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall anoint the altar of burnt offering, with all its vessels, and sanctify the altar: and the altar will be most holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall anoint the basin and its base, and sanctify it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the Tent of Meeting, and shall wash them with water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall put on Aaron the holy garments; and you shall anoint him, and sanctify him, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall bring his sons, and put coats on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall anoint them, as you anointed their father, that they may minister to me in the priest’s office. Their anointing shall be to them for an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses did so. According to all that Yahweh commanded him, so he did.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, the tabernacle was raised up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses raised up the tabernacle, and laid its sockets, and set up its boards, and put in its bars, and raised up its pillars.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spread the covering over the tent, and put the roof of the tabernacle above on it, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took and put the testimony into the ark, and set the poles on the ark, and put the mercy seat above on the ark.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the veil of the screen, and screened the ark of the testimony, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put the table in the Tent of Meeting, on the side of the tabernacle northward, outside of the veil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the bread in order on it before Yahweh, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put the lamp stand in the Tent of Meeting, opposite the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He lit the lamps before Yahweh, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put the golden altar in the Tent of Meeting before the veil;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he burned incense of sweet spices on it, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put up the screen of the door to the tabernacle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the altar of burnt offering at the door of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the meal offering, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and put water therein, with which to wash.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and their feet there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they went into the Tent of Meeting, and when they came near to the altar, they washed, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He raised up the court around the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the screen of the gate of the court. So Moses finished the work.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the cloud covered the Tent of Meeting, and Yahweh’s glory filled the tabernacle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses wasn’t able to enter into the Tent of Meeting, because the cloud stayed on it, and Yahweh’s glory filled the tabernacle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel went onward, throughout all their journeys;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but if the cloud wasn’t taken up, then they didn’t travel until the day that it was taken up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the cloud of Yahweh was on the tabernacle by day, and there was fire in the cloud by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.
